fields.description:
# Add focused prerequisite consistency review

## Goal
Evaluate whether a ticket's plan remains consistent with each direct prerequisite without injecting prerequisite material into every general reviewer or changing existing parent/child review behavior.

## Implementation
- Register a specialized criterion that triggers only after PlanRelationSnapshot collection succeeds and direct prerequisites exist. Unreadable/unresolvable targets stop earlier in pin collection; indeterminate is reserved for readable pinned targets with failed evaluation or attribution.
- Consume the pin story's PlanRelationSnapshot contract; use it only to construct isolated specialized-criterion inputs. Never inject prerequisite material into general finder/verifier calls. Add an integration test proving pin collection plus criterion routing share one preload without prompt leakage.
- Batch multiple independent subject-to-prerequisite evaluations when whole prerequisite blocks fit within the existing context headroom. Never split the subject or a prerequisite.
- Require exactly one record {prerequisite_id, disposition, findings} per canonical ID. disposition is consistent, finding, or indeterminate. consistent has no findings; finding has one or more findings attributed to that same ID; indeterminate carries a reason and cannot block. Missing, duplicate, unknown-ID, or malformed records become indeterminate.
- Keep batching semantically neutral: do not create cross-prerequisite findings whose availability depends on bin membership.
- Give Pass 2 the same isolated subject/prerequisite map so it verifies consistency and attribution. Reuse the phase story's shared Pass-3 threshold function for upheld findings, including the execution floor.
- On a context-limit error retry with smaller bins. When subject plus one prerequisite cannot fit at the largest supported window, produce the existing too-large remediation.
- Extend the existing child-packing fidelity approach with a one-prerequisite-per-call gold baseline. Packed recall must be no lower, false acceptance no higher, per-ID coverage complete, and attribution errors zero, including tail items. If the contract fails, retain smaller bins or one-per-call fallback; batching does not ship degraded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only the focused criterion receives prerequisite material; general criteria and all existing parent/child handling remain unchanged.
- [ ] Token-budget batching preserves whole inputs, produces a disposition for every prerequisite, and cannot block on missing or incorrect canonical-ID attribution.
- [ ] Pass-2 verification, smaller-bin fallback, oversized remediation, and fidelity comparison against one-per-call are covered by deterministic tests plus the established opt-in live evaluation path.

## Verification
- Run focused registry, Pass-1/Pass-2, sizing, and fidelity spot-eval tests.
- Run the existing opt-in prerequisite fidelity evaluation and record its result on the ticket.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
